prose:
Method provenance:
Property: Atom-condensed Fukui function f+ (electrophilic attack index) – headline value refers to the maximum f+ found.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) as implemented in PySCF 2.x via chemkit.
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz; single-point neutral (N), cationic (N-1, charge +1, multiplicity 2) and anionic (N+1, charge –1, multiplicity 2) calculations were run at the same B3LYP/def2-TZVP level – a standard `ΔN=±1` finite-difference protocol (denoted B3LYP/def2-TZVP//acrolein.xyz).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent model).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet reference (charge 0, multiplicity 1); open-shell doublets for the N±1 states as required.
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (backend: PySCF 2.x, ASE 3.x). Density fitting (RI-JK) was active (default in chemkit/PySCF).
Convergence & warnings: All three SCF calculations reported `converged: true`; integrity check status `ok`, trustworthy = true. Warnings emitted by the engine are reproduced verbatim below (they note charge-conservation checks and schema header presence).
Live log & reproducibility: the run streamed live output to a .out file announced at launch (path: fukui_20260702-190806.out); the summarized JSON result is saved as fukui_acrolein_dft.json. Exact command: 
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz --out fukui_acrolein_dft.json
Key results:
• Most electrophilic site: atom 4 (C, index 3) with f⁺ = 0.64232102.
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O, index 0) with f⁻ = 0.39388616.
